summary: Small baselib RObj/JObj matching cleanup. The PR moved the NULL guard for individual-reference increments into the shared iref_INC helper, simplified callers such as HSD_JObjRefThis and HSD_RObjResolveRefs to call iref_INC directly, and adjusted RObj constraint-object helper typing/casts to help match HSD_RObjSetConstraintObj.
what_changed: Changed static inline iref_INC in object.h so it performs the o != NULL check internally before incrementing HSD_OBJ(o)->ref_count_individual and asserting it did not wrap to zero.
Simplified HSD_JObjRefThis in jobj.h from an explicit jobj != NULL guard around iref_INC(jobj) to a direct iref_INC(jobj) call.
Simplified HSD_RObjResolveRefs in robj.c so the resolved robj->u.jobj is passed directly to iref_INC after HSD_ASSERT(883, robj->u.jobj), instead of guarding the call locally.
Changed the local ref_JObj helper signature from HSD_JObj* o to void* o, then cast to HSD_JObj* only for accessing the embedded object field in hsdObjIsDescendantOf.
In HSD_RObjSetConstraintObj, replaced a commented-out HSD_JObj* local with an actual bool isDesc declaration, consistent with descendant-test matching work.
smart_moves: Centralized the NULL behavior in iref_INC rather than duplicating null checks at every call site; this both matches the observed codegen noted in the PR body and reduces future caller-side noise.
Used a direct call pattern for HSD_JObjRefThis and HSD_RObjResolveRefs, relying on iref_INC to preserve safety and produce the expected inline structure.

Changed ref_JObj to accept void* because HSD_RObjSetConstraintObj receives a void* constraint object; delaying the HSD_JObj cast until field access can better reflect the original function's typing and register/codegen behavior.
Preserved the comma-expression descendant check pattern in ref_JObj while adjusting only the type/cast details needed for matching.
decomp_lessons: When multiple callers show the same local NULL guard around an inline helper, verify whether the original SDK-style helper itself contained the guard. Moving the check into the inline can be the matching solution.
For baselib reference-count helpers, caller shape matters: HSD_JObjRefThis matched better as a thin wrapper around iref_INC once iref_INC handled NULL.

For functions that accept generic object pointers, such as HSD_RObjSetConstraintObj, keeping helper parameters as void* and casting only at the field access can avoid introducing non-original typed temporaries.
A matching fix may involve headers as much as C files; inline changes in object.h and jobj.h directly affect code generation in robj.c.
Do not assume a caller-side assert eliminates the need to model helper NULL behavior; HSD_RObjResolveRefs still benefited from calling the NULL-safe helper directly after HSD_ASSERT.
naming_conventions: Kept existing baselib/HSD names: HSD_RObjResolveRefs, HSD_RObjSetConstraintObj, HSD_JObjRefThis, iref_INC, ref_INC.
The helper name ref_JObj remains lower_snake/mixed internal style, while public or exported HSD functions retain HSD_* CamelCase style.
The actual boolean local used for descendant checks is named isDesc, matching the hsdObjIsDescendantOf test result.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Move NULL checks into inline helpers when that is where the original null branch appears; this changes every inlined caller's branch layout.
Prefer direct inline-helper calls at call sites once helper semantics are corrected, rather than wrapping them in redundant if statements.
Use void* parameters and localized casts to influence generated code for generic object APIs.
Retain comma-expression assignment-and-test forms when they are part of the matching shape: assign isDesc from hsdObjIsDescendantOf, then compare isDesc != 0.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching iref_INC or ref_INC to see whether NULL-safe helper semantics were retained.
Search for other callers with redundant iref_INC NULL guards that could be simplified after this change.
Compare subsequent HSD_RObjSetConstraintObj matching work for additional void* versus HSD_JObj* typing lessons.
Audit other HSD object descendant checks for comma-expression isDesc patterns.
